title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei for a Global Studies 201 course.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide introduces Gen Z as digital natives who experience life through an online lens. It highlights high daily usage rates of major social media platforms, framing constant connectivity as an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
Constant connectivity is essentially indispensable in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart comparing screen time usage with mental health outcomes, sourced from a 2025 Mental Health Statistics Report. It shows a link between heavy usage, reported anxiety/sadness, and increased depression risk.
key_claims: 87% of users report usage exceeding 2 hours per day
There is a 70% increase in depression risk factor

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each is presented with an icon and brief explanation of its impact on mental health.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant use of curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide outlines three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital connection: personal digital detox, awareness of platform algorithms, and corporate responsibility for user well-being.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
